sourceMovie.title: Ed Wood
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ed Wood works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ed Wood clicked for you, Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Dallas Buyers Club
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ed Wood clicked for you, Dallas Buyers Club (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ed Wood?
faq.answer: Try Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood, Dallas Buyers Club, Babylon—each shares DNA with Ed Wood while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ed Wood?
faq.answer: Ed Wood (1994) is best described as Comedy, Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ed Wood worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
